title: Disc processing center and laser programmer (unloader)
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_expect: You have previous experience in similar tasks and a good understanding of technical documentation and drawings
Good knowledge of disc manufacturing technology, previous programming experience and disc work center / laser user experience is an advantage
Good Finnish and English language skills. Your English doesn't have to be perfect, but at least you use it boldly
Active, self-directed, systematic approach to work
The work is demanding and sensitive to change, programming is done according to the order backlog. Persistence and pressure tolerance are therefore important qualities in succeeding in the task.
what_we_offer: Everyday work in one shift
A significant role in a growing and stable company
A competent and supportive team around you
The opportunity to develop your skills at the heart of modern production
Salary according to your experience (Technology industry test)
Were you interested?
jobcontent:
Are you interested in working in a growing and developing contract manufacturing company in the metal sector working with versatile steel sheet components?

We are now looking for a plate machining center and laser programmer (engraver) for our work team to strengthen the critical front end of our production. In this role, you can directly influence production efficiency, material use and delivery reliability.

Job duties:

- You play an important role starting from the first stages of our production, determining the timely programming of our sheet machining centers and laser cutting machines, taking into account the best possible material use planning

- In your work, you will not only program our laser and combi machines for cutting, but also

demolition robot of the latest technology

- You will use NC-Express software in your work, (Trutops and JetCam knowledge is an advantage)

What do we expect from you?

- You have previous experience in similar tasks and a good understanding of technical documentation and drawings

- Good knowledge of disc manufacturing technology, previous programming experience and disc work center / laser user experience is an advantage

- Good knowledge of Finnish and English. Your English doesn't have to be perfect, but at least you use it boldly

- Active, self-directed, systematic approach to work

- Cooperation and developing approach

The work is demanding and sensitive to change, programming is done according to the order backlog. Persistence and pressure tolerance are therefore important qualities in succeeding in the task.

What do we offer you?

- Everyday work in one shift

- A significant role in a growing and stable company

- A competent and supportive team around you

- The opportunity to develop your skills at the heart of modern production

- Palkka kokemuksesi mukaan (Teknologiateollisuuden tes)

Were you interested?

Send your application and CV as soon as possible, no later than 24 May 2026. by. We are primarily looking for experts who have already gained experience, but we also consider potential factors and we are interested in e.g. the skills behind operator or machine operation. The work starts according to the agreement, preferably as soon as possible. Laura Rekrytointi helps with recruitment, but the employment relationship is made directly with Steelcomp Vaasa Oy. For more information about the position, please contact Veera Kähäri, veera.kahari@laura.fi

title: Bolt.Works - We're looking for construction professionals to work in Finland, Suomi
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent:
We are recruiting skilled construction professionals from across Europe to work in Finland. We expect applicants to have solid work experience and strong professional skills within the construction industry.
 
You can be a professional in any field, for example concrete work, roofing, scaffolding, or similar. Please specify in your application your work experience, your field of expertise, and describe your skills as clearly as possible, including where you have gained this experience.
 
Work assignments will be agreed upon based on client needs and project timelines, allowing flexibility depending on your availability and preferences. Projects may be located anywhere in Finland, depending on your expertise. Please let us know if you have any location preferences.
 
In your application, please also include:
-When you would be available to start working in Finland
-How long you would be able to work here
-Your salary expectations, along with a brief justification (14-20e/h)
 
Ready to take your construction career to Finland? Send us your application, we look forward to hearing from you!

Bolt.Works on valtakunnallinen henkilöstöpalveluyritys. Olemme reilu työnantaja, ja toimimme apunasi kaikissa urasi vaiheissa. Meillä on hyvä meininki, mahtavat työkaverit sekä alan paras sovellus, jonka kautta hoidat työsuhteeseesi liittyvät asiat parilla klikkauksella. Maksamme aina tehtävää, työkokemustasi ja osaamistasi vastaavan palkan sekä bonusta erinomaisesta työsuorituksesta. Työterveyspalvelut kuuluvat sinulle ensimmäisestä päivästä alkaen. Meillä on tarjota sinulle paljon uravaihtoehtoja ja -polkuja. Asiakkaamme ovat Suomen huippuja eri toimialoilta: teollisuudesta, kaupan alalta, logistiikasta, hotelli- ja ravintola-alalta, rakennusalalta, kiinteistöhuollosta sekä toimistopuolelta. HELA:n ja RT:n jäsenenä toimimme aina yhteisten pelisääntöjen mukaisesti. Tule siis bolttilaisten jengiin, niin olet taatusti parhaassa seurassa!

*

title: Production Operator (fixed-term temporary contract)
company: Easpring Finland New Materials Oy, Kotka
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_expect: monitor the status of a chemical process
jobcategory_keywords: chemical plant and systems monitoring technician
jobcontent:
**Position:** Production Operator

**Location:** Kotka, Finland

**Employment type:** Full-time, fixed-term contract until 01.02.2029

**Position Description:** We are looking for Production Operators with previous experience from a cathode active material (CAM) production plant to join our manufacturing team in Kotka. **Please note** that this position is fixed-term and is needed to ensure that the sample run and commercial production at the Kotka plant are launched successfully. In case you don't have previous experience from a CAM plant, please apply for the other Production Operator position available on our website which doesn't require previous experience from the process industry in a CAM plant.

In this role, you will perform daily production tasks following established plans and procedures, ensuring the highest standards of quality, safety, and efficiency. **In addition**, you are expected to guide and provide hands-on training to Production Operators who don't have previous work experience from the CAM plant

**Key Responsibilities:**

- Operate production equipment and follow Safety Standard Operating Procedures (SSOPs).
 
- Ensure product quality and complete production records accurately.
 
- Guide and provide hands-on training to Production Operators who don't have previous work experience from the CAM plant.
 
- Maintain a clean, organized, and safe work environment adhering to the 6S standards (Sort, Set in Order, Shine, Standardize, Sustain, and Safety).
 
- Meet daily production targets and report any abnormalities promptly.
 
- Handle materials responsibly and support cost control initiatives.
 
- Comply with all Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) requirements.
 

**Qualifications:**

- At least 1-2 years of experience from a similar role within a CAM manufacturer.
 
- English skills are required, other language skills considered to be an asset. Our company language is English.
 
- Ability to follow instructions and quality procedures carefully.
 
- Responsible, safety-minded, and detail-oriented.
 
- Team player with good communication skills.
 

The job tasks in this position correspond to the TVL level of 1 and 2 under the Chemical industry's collective agreement (Kemian perusteollisuuden työehtosopimus).

Join Easpring Finland and be a part of building a new generation of sustainable battery materials manufacturing in Europe!

Easpring Finland is developing a battery materials plant in Kotka, to respond to the rising demand for battery materials in Europe. The plant will produce cathode active material, which is needed to manufacture batteries for electric vehicles.

title: Kindergarten director
salary_range: OVTES:n mukainen
jobcontent:
The director of the daycare center is responsible for managing the early childhood education unit in accordance with pedagogical, financial and operational goals. The daycare manager is the immediate supervisor in two small one- and two-group daycare centers in Miekkarinte and Tuulentuva, and he is also responsible for supervising family daycare. Viitasaari has two group family daycare units and a few family daycare workers in the beginning of the operating period.

As a daycare manager, you participate and influence the early childhood education management team. 

We appreciate an enthusiastic pedagogical and administrative management professional. The director of the daycare center has the skill and courage to develop the skills of the staff and involve them. You know how to organize, make decisions, your way of interacting is constructive, solution-oriented and positive. Experience as a daycare manager is considered an advantage.

The eligibility requirement is the eligibility requirement of the daycare manager according to § 31 of the Early Childhood Education Act 540/2018, however, taking into account the entry into force and transition provisions of § 74 and § 75.

Salary and other terms of employment are determined according to OVTES. 

The application must be submitted electronically by 11:59 p.m. on May 15, 2026 at the address KuntaRekry.fi.
Or alternatively by letter to the address: 
Viitasaari early childhood education, Keskitie 10, 44500 Viitasaari.

The selected person must submit an acceptable medical certificate about their health and present a criminal record extract in accordance with the Act on the Investigation of the Criminal Background of Persons Working with Children (504/2002) within 30 days of being notified of the selection decision. 
The position has a 6-month trial period.

Viitasaari is a cozy and modern rural town on the shore of Lake Keitele in Lake Finland.
Viitasaari values ​​both civility and inventive entrepreneurship. We offer the city's employees an interesting job, an ePassi well-being benefit, and good support from the work community.

Viitasaari's early childhood education staffing and support resources are good. Welcome to our cozy and lively city as a daycare manager.

Maria Kilpeläinen, director of early childhood education
Phone 040 1371694

title: Boat fitter
salary_range: Palkka ja palkkakehitys kokemuksen ja osaamisen perusteella
what_we_expect: to define fault from faulty engines
what_we_offer: Workplace at Finland's finest shipyard in modern facilities
The latest tools and modern boat handling equipment
Great work atmosphere and motivated colleagues
As experience accumulates, an increasingly responsible job description
The opportunity to develop with our organization
jobcontent:
You are part of a professional team where you are given responsibility and the freedom to handle your own tasks independently.

The job description includes the following tasks:

1. Maintenance and repairs of boats and their engines and peripherals
 
2. Lifting, moving and launching boats
 
3. Maintenance of lifting and moving equipment
 

We expect the following from the person we apply for:

- Practical experience working with boats
 
- Knowledge of technical systems and/or structures of boats
 
- Self-initiative and flexibility
 
- Willingness for a variety of work tasks
 

In addition, we value the following:

- Experience in transporting and servicing work machines
 

We offer:

- Workplace at Finland's finest shipyard in modern facilities
 
- Latest tools and modern boat handling equipment
 
- Great work atmosphere and motivated colleagues
 
- As experience accumulates, an increasingly responsible job description
 
- The opportunity to develop with our organization

_Hanko Boat Yard is a winter boat storage and maintenance company operating in the southern tip of Finland, known for its high customer satisfaction and high-quality service._

_10,000 m2 warm, modern and bright hall with all amenities, and our professional staff take care of almost 200 boats of our customers. Our 1200m2 maintenance and repair hall, which is connected to the storage hall, can also carry out larger and more challenging maintenance and repair work._

_In addition to winter storage for our customers' boats, we also offer all boat maintenance performed by our own competent staff. Motor and electronic installations, carpentry, fiberglass repairs, washing, polishing and waxing._

_All of course with respect for the environment and nature._

_We are also an authorized_ **_Volvo Penta_**_and_ **_Yanmar_** _service point and_ **_Targa Service Partner,_** _but we have long experience with all engine and boat brands._

For more information, please contact the CEO of Hanko Boat Yard, Jonas Slotte, **jonas.slotte@hby.fi** or by phone on weekdays**Mon-Tues from 9 am to 4 pm** at the number **040-5873600**.

Free-form applications by **31 May 2026** to **info@hby.fi.**

jobapply_link: mailto:teemu@vaiha.fi?subject=Application for the Job - Keittiön vuoromestari

what_we_expect: comply with food-related health, safety, hygiene and other relevant legislation
take care of the correct temperature of the kitchen appliances
jobcontent:
Vaiha Oy is a Mikkeliläinen family-owned restaurant business. Vaiha restaurants include Vaiha Asema at the Mikkeli railway station, Vaiha Elias in Mikkeli's know-how quarter in Kaukola, as well as the restaurant Pormestari and the summer terrace restaurant Pikku Pormestari in the courtyard of Mikkeli's town hall. We prefer ingredients from local producers in the lunch, order and seasonal menus. Catering is a growing part of our business.

Your workplace is primarily the restaurant Pormestari or Vaiha Elias, but depending on the situation, work is also done in other workplaces and in catering operations.

As a kitchen supervisor, you are responsible for maintaining the quality of the restaurant's kitchen and organizing kitchen work in your own shift in cooperation with other personnel. In order to be successful in this job, you should have an education in the field as well as previous experience as a front-line worker and strong knowledge of practical work. You must also have know-how in purchasing, loss and margin management. A good working atmosphere is important to us, so you lead your shift with your own positive energy and example.

We offer a versatile work environment and varied tasks, an extensive occupational health care service, and leisure accident insurance. Become part of a professional and developing team!

For more information about the job, please contact restaurateur Teemu Kaijanen on 040 8393292
Applications and CVs to toimisto@vaiha.fi

As a kitchen supervisor, you are responsible for maintaining the quality of the restaurant's kitchen and organizing kitchen work in your own shift in cooperation with other personnel. In order to be successful in this job, you should have an education in the field as well as previous experience as a front-line worker and strong knowledge of practical work. You must also have know-how in purchasing, loss and margin management. A good working atmosphere is important to us, so you lead your shift with your own positive energy and example.

We offer a versatile work environment and varied tasks, an extensive occupational health care service, and leisure accident insurance. Become part of a professional and developing team!

For more information about the job, please contact restaurateur Teemu Kaijanen on 040 8393292
Applications and CVs to toimisto@vaiha.fi

title: Special class teacher
salary_range: Lönen och övriga villkor för anställningsförhållandet bestäms enligt UKTA.
jobcontent:
We are looking for a special class teacher for Höjdens school for the period 1.8.2026-31.7.2027. We are looking for someone who is positive and empathetic, cooperative and solution-oriented to work in Oasen, a concept we have with small group teaching alongside the general teaching in grades 1-6. You are a committed teacher who wants to create good conditions for students' learning, and who can design individual learning paths and work goal-oriented in small and flexible groups. You support individual students' academic success and promote students' well-being in everyday school life through security, encouragement and clear routines. You consider the different needs of students and can work in close collaboration with different colleagues. You use IT as a natural tool in teaching. 

What we can offer you:
- Opportunity for training
- Employee benefits (exercise, culture, etc.)
- Collegial support / pleasant working community

Eligibility requirements:
- Authorization in accordance with regulation (986/1998)
- Suitability for the position
- Criminal record extract (504/2002) before starting employment

The basic salary for a qualified applicant for the position is €3300 (special class teacher with master's degree), with the possibility of other supplements according to UKTA and other supplements based on the difficulty of the task. The salary depends on the qualification level.
We apply a trial period of 6 months. Certificate of health status is presented during the first month in service.

We look forward to your application! 
Submit your application via Kuntarekry no later than 13.5.2026 at 12:00.

Höjden's school is a unit school with grades 1-9 with approx. 180 students and there are opportunities to work flexibly across the stage boundaries. In our school, we have a strong spirit where we work together for the good of the children and young people.

jobcontent:
School counselor, become part of the Luolaja school community to guide students in everyday school life. Luolaja's school is a single-class school with about 100 students. We are looking for a temporary school counselor for 10 August 2026-5 June 2027. The reason for the temporary nature is the instability of the operation.

The school counselor, together with the principal, is responsible for the afternoon activities at the Luolaja school. Daily working hours are around 12:00-17:00, a total of 20 hours/week.

The task includes the planning, implementation and cooperation with families of afternoon club activities. Sometimes you get to work as a work partner of another afternoon club instructor.

You have good communication skills and a desire to cooperate. Important skills in everyday school life are flexibility, creativity and the ability to meet and support different children. In your work, you may answer e.g. about monitoring the medical treatment of a diabetic child.

When you become part of the people of Hämeenlinna, you get:

* flexible working opportunities that support balancing work and free time
* nice and competent colleagues
* a team where everyone can be themselves and where we help each other
* grow into an expert in your field
* opportunities for meaningful work and influence
* enabling management to support your own career
* occupational health services that support your well-being and health
* Epass welfare balance
* extensive wellness and exercise services and benefits aimed at personnel
* develop, experiment and stumble
* developer-friendly working community
* employer support for skill development and training

In the working culture of the people of Hämeenlinna, we value the diversity of the working community and work for equality. With us, everyone gets to be themselves. We welcome applicants from different backgrounds, such as people of different ages, different genders, and representatives of different population, language and cultural groups.

This recruitment is carried out as an anonymous recruitment promoting equality. Applications are processed in the first stage of the recruitment process without identifying job applicants such as name, picture, date of birth, gender, contact information or native language. Avoid revealing identification information in the open questions of the application form. The anonymity of the search ends when the decision on those invited to the recruitment discussion has been made. Please enter all relevant information on the application form. The information in the CV is only available to the recruiting line manager after a decision has been made on who will be invited to the recruitment interview.

We want to be a pioneer in promoting the well-being of the personnel and have invested in promoting the physical lifestyle of the personnel in many different ways. As proof of this, the city of Hämeenlinna has received the Finland's Most Active Workplace certificate.

title: Commercial Manager
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent:
Enersense Engineering is currently seeking Commercial Managers to support our clients Data Centre projects in several locations across Finland. We are hiring on a permanent basis and the work is set to start as soon as possible.

Main tasks and responsibilities:
 * Lead and manage the commercial team in a fast-paced construction environment, ensuring high performance, accountability and strong commercial awareness
 * Put effective cost control processes in place
 * Prepare and present key commercial reports to senior management
 * Plan and manage project procurement to meet timelines, scope and budget
 * Ensure a smooth handover of information from pre-construction to the project team
 * Build and maintain good relationships with clients and suppliers
 * Ensure all commercial documentation is accurate and properly maintained
 * Monitor project risks and opportunities together with the project team
 * Identify and address commercial issues early
 * Carry out due diligence on new subcontractors and suppliers, including checking their capacity before engagement
 * Ensure subcontract and purchase order documentation is complete and protects the companys interests
 * Manage payment processes in line with contract terms, including client payments and supply chain invoicing

Requirements:
 * Degree in Quantity Surveying or a related field
 * 10+ years of relevant experience
 * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal
 * Good numerical and problem-solving skills
 * Experience working for a main contractor
 * Knowledge of MEP is an advantage
 * Experience with different procurement methods (e.g. lump sum, remeasure, two-stage / open book, design & build)
 * Previous experience in Data Centre projects is mandatory
 * Finnish language skills considered as a benefit

Please send your application soon, as the position will be filled as soon as a suitable candidate is found. Due to GDPR regulations, applications will only be accepted through our official application system.

Please note that due to clients urgent need only applicants with a valid working permit / EU-citizenship can be considered.

We manage this recruitment process internally and therefore do not require support from external recruitment agencies.

For further information, please contact: Terhi Uusimäki, Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist, +358 40 569 5300, terhi.uusimaki@enersense.com

title: Mechanical Construction Manager
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent:
Enersense Engineering is currently seeking Mechanical Construction Managers to support our clients Data Centre projects in several locations across Finland. We are hiring on a permanent basis and the work is set to start as soon as possible.

Main tasks and responsibilities:
 * Manage communication with the client
 * Prepare method statements and risk assessments for construction activities and ensure HVAC work meets project and safety requirements
 * Coordinate with the project team to schedule pipework installation according to the project timeline
 * Review the project at handover, manage procurement of equipment and subcontractors, and oversee technical purchasing
 * Assist in documenting and pricing scope changes and preparing monthly valuations and final accounts
 * Support the project schedule, including preparing a two-week look-ahead plan with the project team
 * Ensure sufficient labour resources are available according to the project schedule
 * Ensure all compliance and governance requirements are followed, especially:
 + Site health and safety, ensuring subcontractors work safely
 + Identifying improvements to working methods to increase safety
 + Maintaining good housekeeping standards on site

Requirements:
 * Relevant mechanical qualification (e.g., Engineering degree, City & Guilds, HNC/BTEC, or NVQ)
 * Experience in a similar role within a mechanical and electrical contracting environment is preferred
 * Data Centre project experience is an advantage
 * Good organisational, communication and time management skills
 * Previous experience in Data Centre projects is mandatory
 * Finnish language skills considered as a benefit

Due to GDPR regulations, applications will only be accepted through our official application system

title: Electrical Construction Manager
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent:
Enersense Engineering is currently seeking Electrical Construction Managers to support our clients Data Centre projects in several locations across Finland. We are hiring on a permanent basis and the work is set to start as soon as possible.

Main tasks and responsibilities:
 * Manage site Quality Assurance in coordination with the QA Manager, ensuring all works meet the highest standards
 * Oversee the project schedule with the Project Team and prepare two-week look-aheads for upcoming works
 * Ensure labour resources are planned and maintained in line with project requirements
 * Supervise subcontractor activities, monitoring progress against schedules and manpower trackers
 * Identify and report all variations and delays to Project Management
 * Verify that all plant and materials delivered are in line with specifications and safely stored onsite
 * Support project close-out, including O&M manuals, punch lists, safety files, QA files, and material handovers, in collaboration with the Site Project Manager
 * Uphold compliance and governance standards, including:
 * Enforcing Health & Safety requirements and promoting safe working practices
 * Driving continuous safety improvements and ensuring high housekeeping standards
 * Collaborate with Package Managers to develop and implement company procedures
 * Manage change control effectively, ensuring cost and programme impacts are captured and addressed
 * This role offers the opportunity to take ownership of critical electrical construction activities while contributing to the successful delivery of major projects

Requirements:
 * Relevant Electrical Qualification essential eg. City & Guilds Certificate, HNC/BTEC or NVQ
 * 5+ years of previous experience in a similar role, electrical construction, preferably within large-scale projects such as data centres, industrial, pharmaceutical, or mission-critical facilities
 * Strong knowledge of LV/MV electrical systems, QA, and safety compliance
 * Proven ability to manage subcontractors, labour, schedules, and costs
 * Experience with change control, variations, and project reporting
 * Proficiency in MS Project (or similar tools)
 * Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
 * Previous experience in Data Centre projects is mandatory
 * Finnish language skills considered as a benefit

 * 5+ years of previous experience in a similar role, electrical construction, preferably within large-scale projects such as data centres, industrial, pharmaceutical, or mission-critical facilities
 * Strong knowledge of LV/MV electrical systems, QA, and safety compliance
 * Proven ability to manage subcontractors, labour, schedules, and costs
 * Experience with change control, variations, and project reporting
 * Proficiency in MS Project (or similar tools)
 * Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
 * Previous experience in Data Centre projects is mandatory
 * Finnish language skills considered as a benefit

Please send your application soon, as the position will be filled as soon as a suitable candidate is found. Due to GDPR regulations, applications will only be accepted through our official application system.

Please note that due to clients urgent need only applicants with a valid working permit / EU-citizenship can be considered.

We manage this recruitment process internally and therefore do not require support from external recruitment agencies.

For further information, please contact: Terhi Uusimäki, Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist, +358 40 569 5300, terhi.uusimaki@enersense.com

We have helped our clients to employ over 3000 specialists in various projects across 40 countries globally.

title: Local nurse Hearing Centre, Surgical Hospital
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent:
Hello, you nurse! Are you looking for a versatile day job that combines guiding patients of different ages and their close circle, manual skills, independent reception work and working in a multi-professional team? This could be the perfect opportunity for you!

We are looking for a personal nurse for the Hearing Center for a fixed-term employment until 31 December 2026. Start of work according to the agreement.

You work as a doctor's partner and take care of other tasks of a nurse. Later on, you will have the opportunity to familiarize yourself with, for example, group guidance of patients, sampling of earpieces, repairs and maintenance work.

Above all, good communication skills are needed in the work, because the focus is guiding and counseling patients. In addition, we value manual skills, technical know-how and knowledge of the anatomy of the ear and the physiology of hearing. You will meet our patients of all ages and their families in different life situations.

Audiological nursing is a constantly developing field, and in this job you have good opportunities to influence the development of your own work and skills together with the hearing center's multiprofessional team. We value a positive attitude and a desire to learn new things. We invest in your orientation, because the job cannot be learned in weeks. We consider previous experience in reception activities an advantage.

Do you want to do your part to influence the functional capacity and quality of life of our hearing impaired patients? Take another look at Kuulotalo: https://www.terveyskyla.fi/kuulotalo/palvelut/hus-kuulokeskusku-palvelut

We start the interviews already during the search. Recruitment is carried out for video and live interviews. So submit your application as soon as possible.

The job-specific salary is €2716.25/month and possible experience and other bonuses.

At HUS, you are part of the most effective treatment.

Job eligibility conditions

Nursing degree or basic nursing degree.

We require vaccination protection in accordance with the Infectious Diseases Act from those working in customer and patient facilities, and a health report on pulmonary tuberculosis from those working in healthcare operational units.

Contact information

Department manager Henna Kaipainen, tel. 0504287658.

title: Laboratory nurse Diagnostic Center KFI Peijas
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
what_we_offer: a comprehensive orientation
opportunity for professional development
in the form of sports, cultural and commuting benefits to ePass
employment apartments for rent
nice and professional colleagues

The job-specific salary for a laboratory nurse is €2957.14/month. In addition, it is possible to get a work experience bonus of a maximum of 8%. The HUS Diagnostic Center uses performance pay, which makes it possible to increase the monthly earnings.
Eligibility criteria for the assignment:
Licensed laboratory nurse.

We require vaccination protection in accordance with the Infectious Diseases Act from those working in customer and patient facilities, and a health report on pulmonary tuberculosis from those working in healthcare operational units.
Contact information
jobcontent:
Welcome to Peijas hospital's Clinical Physiology as a laboratory nurse!

HUS Diagnostic Center is Finland's leading producer of clinical laboratory and imaging services. Our main area of ​​operation is the HUS group's specialized hospital care and the primary care clients of the welfare areas under HUS' responsibility.

We are nationally responsible for several studies that require special expertise, as well as for research and teaching belonging to a university hospital. Diagnostic tests play a key role in confirming and ruling out diseases, as part of treatment, and in monitoring and screening treatment. Approximately 3,300 laboratory and imaging specialists work in more than 150 locations at the HUS Diagnostic Center.

We are looking for a laboratory nurse at the Diagnostic Center for a fixed-term employment 1 June 2026 8 August 2027 in the clinical physiology unit of Peijas Hospital.

As a laboratory nurse, you play a central role in ensuring the implementation of high-quality and patient-safe research. In your position, you get to work in a versatile way with customer advice and various researches. Your duties include, among other things, ECG and RR long-term registrations, spirometry studies, ECG pre-analytics, endurance tests in the upright position, stress studies, diffusion capacity measurements and manometry studies.

We offer you versatile and responsible work as part of a professional team and the opportunity to develop your skills in interesting and meaningful work.

With these characteristics, you have the best conditions for success in the task:
 * good interaction and teamwork skills
 * flexibility and pressure tolerance
 * positive and enthusiastic attitude towards customer service
 * fast and efficient takeover of work tasks

We require good oral and written Finnish language skills and commitment to our goals.

We offer you:
 * a comprehensive orientation
 * opportunity for professional development
 * in the form of exercise, cultural and commuting benefit to ePass
 * employee apartments for rent
 * nice and professional colleagues

The job-specific salary for a laboratory nurse is €2957.14/month. In addition, it is possible to get a work experience bonus of a maximum of 8%. The HUS Diagnostic Center uses performance pay, which makes it possible to increase the monthly earnings.

Start of work June 1, 2026 or according to agreement. We are already interviewing during the application period. Welcome to our colleague!

Eligibility criteria for the assignment:

Licensed laboratory nurse.

We require vaccination protection in accordance with the Infectious Diseases Act from those working in customer and patient facilities, and a health report on pulmonary tuberculosis from those working in healthcare operational units.

Contact information

Department manager Petteri Saaristola phone 040 586 2775.

title: Bartender
salary_range: Sopimuksen mukaan
jobcontent:
**_Bartender Beer restaurant Solmuun_**

_Olutravintola Solmu is a sailor-inspired and relaxed pub in Vuosaari's Aurinkolahti. We are located right by the sea, and our wide selection of beers and our wonderful terrace attract visitors from near and far. Our daily life is versatile: in addition to quality beers, the house also has board games, trivia quizzes and stand-up clubs in Kieli Solmu._

_We are now looking for a full-time bar waiter to join us_**_(112.5 h / 3 weeks)._**

**_Employee about us_**

**_Working environment:_**_You will work in one of Helsinki's most amazing landscapes. In summer, the focus is on the large sun terrace, in winter, in atmospheric interiors and event nights._

**_Working hours:_**_Work shifts are emphasized_**_evenings and weekends_**_,_

**_Work community:_**_We are a small and tight-knit group, where we value the direct attitude of doing things, good humor and blowing coal together._

**_We expect from you_**

**_Skills:_**_We mainly work alone, so you must be of legal age and have already gained experience working at a bar/or in another restaurant industry. Dispensing and hygiene passports are a prerequisite._

**_Ability to serve:_**_You enjoy meeting people, your smile doesn't freeze even when you're in a hurry, and you know how to keep things together even when the terrace is full.

**_Interest in the field:_**_You know beers or are at least willing to learn something new from our wide selection._

**_Additional information and search_**

_The employment is full-time and starts according to the contract. When hiring, we take into account your experience and education. If you are the customer service professional we are looking for, send a free-form application with salary expectations and CV to the address:anni.lauren@onniravintolat.fi no later than May 24. However, be quick, because applications are already processed during the application period and the position will be filled when a suitable person is found._

The story of Onnira restaurants began in 1996, when Sher Jang Singh founded his first restaurant in Espoo. Since then, the family business has grown steadily and is currently a successful entity comprising thirteen different restaurants. The selection includes, among other things, restaurants serving traditional food and special beers, pubs, live music venues, karaoke bars and nightclubs.

Onniravintolat is a responsible family business piloted by the second generation, which has steadily grown by establishing new restaurants. Growth is also a strong part of Onniravintoloite's vision for the future, and the company is constantly looking for new restaurants and business premises as part of its functioning restaurant world.

Passion for restaurant operations and careful listening to customers' wishes have been seen as successes. This is also supported by a committed team of nearly a hundred own employees, who professionally manage different restaurant concepts. Light organization and investment in good management enable quick reaction in changing situations.

**Job Title:** Bartender

**Job description:**

**_Bartender Beer restaurant Solmuun_**

_Olutravintola Solmu is a sailor-inspired and relaxed pub in Vuosaari's Aurinkolahti. We are located right by the sea, and our wide selection of beers and our wonderful terrace attract visitors from near and far. Our daily life is versatile: in addition to quality beers, you can enjoy board games, trivia quizzes and the Kieli Solmussa stand-up club in the house._

_We are now looking for a full-time bar waiter (112.5 h / 3 weeks)._**

**_About work and us_**

**_Working environment:_** _You will work in one of the most amazing landscapes in Helsinki. In summer, the focus is on the large sun terrace, in winter, in atmospheric interiors and event nights._

**_Working hours:_** _Shifts are emphasized_ **_evenings and weekends_**_,_

**_Work community:_** _We are a small and tight-knit group, where we value the direct attitude of doing things, good humor and working together._

**_We expect from you_**

**_Skills:_** _We generally work alone, so you must be of legal age and have already gained experience working at a bar and/or in another restaurant industry. Dispensing and hygiene passports are a prerequisite._

**_Ability to serve:_** _You enjoy meeting people, your smile doesn't freeze even when you're in a hurry and you know how to keep things together even when the terrace is full. Table setting is not a foreign thing to you._

**_Interest in the field:_** _You know beer or are at least willing to learn something new from our wide selection._

**_Additional information and search_**

_The employment is full-time and starts according to the contract. When hiring, we take your experience and education into account. If you are the customer service professional we are looking for, send a free-form application with salary expectations and CV no later than 24.5. to: anni.lauren@onniravintolat.fi. However, be quick, because applications are already processed during the application period and the position will be filled when a suitable person is found._

**Occupations:** Waiter

**Competences:** Hygiene pass, Alcoholic drink pass

**Salary:** Hourly salary, according to agreement

**Application instructions:**

For more information, contact restaurant manager Anni Lauré by email. Free-form applications with CV and salary expectations to: anni.lauren@onniravintolat.fi.

**Application ends on 24 May 2026 Company presentation:**

Solmu beer restaurant is Aurinkolahti's own living room pub with a sailor spirit and a warm spirit, right in the heart of Vuosaari's seashore. Our location next to the marina and the beach offers our customers one of Helsinki's most spectacular views, and on our large sun terrace you can entertain well into the evening.

title: Dentist Private dental clinics in Eastern Finland
salary_range: Tuntipalkka- tai prosenttipalkkamalli
jobcontent:
We are surveying dentists interested in jobs located in Eastern Finland. The locations can be, for example, Mikkeli, Savonlinna or Imatra. We will contact you as soon as places open up!

The job description includes versatile reception work, so-called general dentist job description. We are looking for committed and flexible workers. The dentist should also be ready to work 1-2 evening shifts per week. Saturday shifts are possible, but not mandatory. In these jobs, the dentist can choose either a percentage salary model or an hourly salary model according to his wishes.

The dentist's Finnish language skills must be very good. We require a language certificate and the level should be C2.

As an employee of MediKaisla, you have the support of the top team from the beginning of the recruitment process to the end of the employment relationship.

Below is a summary of the job opportunity: Dentist Private dental clinics in Eastern Finland

Job description: Dentist's reception work at a private dental practice.
Working time: Negotiable, morning/day shifts + 1-2 evening shifts per week.
Start: Negotiable.
Duration: Until now, negotiable.
Remuneration: Total hourly wage (incl. holiday pay and money) or percentage wage model.
Job requirements: Licensed dentist, Finnish language skills C2.

Were you interested? Apply for a position using the "Apply for a position" button below.

Or ask for more information:
The MediKaisla team
029 170 1370
medi@eilakaisla.fi

- Licensed dentist

- Very good Finnish language skills, C2

MediKaisla

title: Basic education lesson teacher, household and health information
salary_range: OVTES:n mukaan
job_responsibilities: teacher, upper grades and high school
jobcontent:
The Kivelä school in Leppävirta municipality has an open position for a primary education home economics and health information teacher for the period August 1, 2026 - March 12, 2027. The task includes teaching home economics and health information to grades 7-9. 
We value up-to-date information and communication technology skills, good interaction skills, as well as your own vision and willingness to develop teaching and the school community, as well as the ability to work in a team. 
Salary according to OVTES. 
The position requires the qualification according to the qualification regulation 986/1998 for teaching personnel. 
Before the selection is confirmed, the person selected for the position must present a criminal record extract in accordance with § 3 of the Act on investigating the criminal background of those working with children (504/2002) and an acceptable medical certificate about their health
The position is subject to a three (3) month trial period.

Leppävirta is a vibrant and modern residential and business municipality of approx. 9,000 inhabitants.
It is located along highway 5 and Saimaa's deep channel, as the southern neighbor of Kuopio. Leppävirta offers a safe and comfortable living environment with good services. 

The municipality of Leppävirta is a multidisciplinary employer, which as one of the largest local employers employs around 300 people. Our values ​​are transparency, fairness and responsibility. The municipality is a smoke-free employer and we have received UNICEF's child-friendly municipality recognition.

For more information, contact principal Niina Häkkinen on 040 8206546

title: Director of facility services
what_we_offer: Conditions for success

The eligibility criteria for the position are a suitable higher education degree, management experience and excellent Finnish, good English and satisfactory oral and written Swedish. If the university degree has been completed abroad, a degree equivalency decision is required.

In the city, the overall salary system applies to management positions. The determination of the salary is essentially influenced by the demandingness of the task as well as the person's leadership ability and performance.
jobcontent:
If you want to play a central role in developing space solutions for education and training in Helsinki, come to us as a space service manager! Are you excited about the management and responsibility of large entities? We are looking for a manager to be responsible for the whole of the industry's spatial services. 

As facility service director, you are responsible for the strategic planning, development and management of the facilities used by the education and training sector. In this role, you lead the expert organization of spatial services and ensure that the functional and economic spatial solutions are suitable for learners of different ages and for resident use. As a manager, you will cooperate extensively with the users of the premises and the city's various organizations and stakeholders in, for example, space projects and leasing, project management, service network planning and regional development projects. In addition, your tasks include the preparation of presentations and statements for senior management decision-making, as well as acting as a presenter in management groups and as an expert in the meetings of the board of education and training. 

You manage the learning space design services service of around 30 experts, which is divided into three sub-units; to the service network, space projects, and security services unit. As the director of the service, you manage operations, personnel and finances, you are responsible for achieving performance and goals, as well as developing operations. 

Design services for learning spaces is responsible for determining the industry's need for service space, developing the service network, and renting and contract management of spaces for resident use. In addition, the design services of the learning spaces are responsible for the guidance, development, guidance and support of the functionality and bulk furnishing of the design of the space projects. The service is partly responsible for the promotion and coordination of safety issues in the industry. In addition, managing preparedness and continuity in the industry also belongs to the planning service of learning spaces. 

The cornerstones of managing the city of Helsinki are clearly visible in your work, which are vision, consistency, enthusiasm and executive ability. Your work is guided by the promise of managing our industry, which is built around community control, responsibility and locality. 

What do we offer you? 

You get to develop Helsinki's learning environments far into the future and you work in a competent and enthusiastic community of experts. By working together, we find the best solutions for the benefit of our customers. You belong to the active and collaborative management team of the industry's shared services, consisting of top professionals. With us, you get comprehensive employment benefits, such as commuting, exercise and lunch benefits. You have wonderful working facilities in Kalasatama. 

Conditions for success 

We require experience in space planning and construction. In addition, you have the ability to manage a large space network and investments in a cost-effective way in the long term, and connect space solutions to the industry's core mission - supporting learning and education. You have good leadership and front-person skills, the ability to make independent decisions, and goal-oriented activities, taking into account broad entities. We require excellent cooperation and negotiation skills as well as the ability to function smoothly in the municipal organization. 

The eligibility criteria for the position are a suitable higher education degree, management experience and excellent Finnish, good English and satisfactory oral and written Swedish. If the university degree has been completed abroad, a degree equivalency decision is required. 

The position has a six-month probationary period. Before accepting the position, the chosen one must present an acceptable report on his health status. In addition, with the consent of the applicant, a personal security clearance is conducted on the person to be selected for the position (security clearance act 726/2014). You can find more detailed information about the procedure at https://supo.fi/turvallissiselvitykset (link takes you to an external service). 

In the city, the overall salary system applies to management positions. The determination of the salary is essentially influenced by the demandingness of the task as well as the person's leadership ability and performance. 

We will implement the first phase as a video interview, for which selections will be made in week 21. Based on the video interviews, we will select the applicants for the first round of live interviews, which will be conducted on June 1. and 15.6. We keep applicants informed about the progress of recruitment throughout the process. Leave your application via the attached Apply for a position link on 14.5. by 3:00 p.m., the application period has been extended and those who applied earlier will be considered in the application.

To promote equality and equality, the City of Helsinki encourages anonymous recruitment, and this recruitment is carried out anonymously. The goal of anonymous recruitment is to focus attention on the applicant's professional skills and work experience. Fill out the job application form normally. When writing the application, note that it follows the principles of anonymity. The supervisor selects the persons to be interviewed based on an anonymous summary and does not show the applicant's name, age, gender or native language.

For us, every day is the most important in the world! 

High-quality education and training are the foundation of a functioning city and a good life. We are Finland's largest and most influential organizer of early childhood education, pre-primary and primary education, upper secondary and vocational education and free educational work. With the power of 15,000 experts, we are building a lifelong learning path for city dwellers. 

We want to be the capital of education and training and a trendsetter in pedagogy. We share our know-how and boldly develop our work together. We consider it important to balance work and free time. With us, everyone can be themselves! 

Come with us to grow tomorrow's Helsinki!

Get to know us at hel.fi/edurekry and on social media #helsinkioppii

finance and planning director Tero Vuontisjärvi, phone 050 - 538 4643. Telephone hours Thu 16.4. from 10:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. and Mon 20.4. from 12:00 to 14:00.

jobcontent:
We are looking for construction support workers for our client companies in the Oulu region for various long-term employment relationships. The work begins according to the contract, depending on the needs of our client companies, and the duration of employment varies from a few days to months. As a construction support worker, your job description includes assisting tasks in the construction industry, such as assisting with protection and building cleaning and carrying tasks in new and renovated sites. title: Early childhood education special assistant
jobcategory_keywords: assistant in early childhood education and care
jobcontent:
We are looking for a special assistant for Lope's early childhood education, Päiväkoti Pellava, for a fixed-term employment for the period 1 August 2026 - 31 July 2027. 

As a special assistant, you promote the child's independence, independent survival and participation in the group's activities. Support can be directed to one or more children. The assistant supports and guides the child in daily situations as part of the early childhood education/preschool team and also receives strong support from the early childhood education special teacher. Working in the position requires a sensitive, positive and, if necessary, firm approach in accordance with attendance pedagogy, as well as good interaction and teamwork skills. 

The special assistant's working hours are the average general working hours (38.25 hours/week) and the working hours are determined according to the childcare time of the assisted child. 

Lope municipality's personnel benefits include e.g. €100 work clothing allowance/year for early childhood education employees, ePass massage or sports/cultural benefits and benefits offered by the municipality's sports services. Mehiläinen is responsible for the municipality's occupational health services. 

We expect the person selected for the position to have an education in the field (e.g. a professional degree in education and guidance) and work experience in the field. Salary is determined according to the municipal general employment and collective agreement (KVTES). The standard salary for a special assistant in the municipality of Lope is currently €2245.55/month. 

The person selected must present a criminal record extract in accordance with the Act on investigating the criminal background of those working with children and young people (504/2002), and a report on the respiratory tuberculosis of those working with children under school age will be made and checked (Infectious Diseases Act § 55) in the interview situation. As a tuberculosis questionnaire, the questionnaire prepared by the National Institute of Occupational Health and the interpretation instructions are used https://www.ttl.fi/teemat/tyoterveys/pandemiat-ja-epidemiat/tuberkuloosiin-liittyvat-terveysverkeidenset. 

The position is subject to a six (6) month probationary period. 

Those invited to the interview will be asked to see the original eligibility certificates. We may start interviews already during the application process. 

We ask that applications be submitted primarily electronically at www.kuntarekry.fi by 12:00 on May 15, 2026.

Loppi is the southernmost of the Kanta-Häme province, a vibrant municipality of just under 8,000 inhabitants, which employs around 400 employees. The municipality of Lope is a reliable employer that takes care of the well-being of its personnel and offers its personnel a good job at all stages of their career and life.

Loppi is a smoke-free employer.

For more information, please contact the daycare manager, Johanna Jalli-Karimäki, phone 040 330 6118 or email firstname.surname@loppi.fi

title: Maintenance specialist, relay protection
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent: At Helen, we are renewing ourselves vigorously and constantly developing cleaner and more environmentally friendly ways of producing energy. We want to lead the energy revolution as a trend setter for the green transition and offer new regional and renewable energy solutions that are increasingly better suited to our customers' needs. New innovations are part of our everyday life, and they are born in cooperation with our customers. At Helen Sähköverko, we ensure a smooth everyday life for the people of Helsinki. Our electricity grid is the most reliable in Europe: a Helsinki resident experiences a half-hour power cut on average only every ten years. We maintain and develop Helsinki's nearly 6,500 km electricity network efficiently. Helen Sähköverkko is facing an unprecedented challenge as the clean transition doubles Helsinki's electricity consumption. A clean transition and the development of the city can also be seen in the significant growth of our investment program. Enabling growth requires moving to a 400 kV voltage level in our region's electricity transmission, which creates a completely new challenge for the development of the electricity network. Helen Sähköverkko is part of the Helen Group, which in addition to heat, cooling and electricity offers new solutions for regional and renewable energy, smart real estate and electric transport that are increasingly better suited to our customers' needs. In order to achieve our ambitious goals in enabling a clean transition and to serve our customers even better, we are now looking for a solution-seeking and development-minded maintenance expert who is an iron professional in electrical network relay protection and secondary systems to join us for a permanent employment relationship. As a maintenance expert specializing in relay protection and secondary systems, you are responsible for the definition, maintenance and development of the relay protection of the electrical network, and you analyze the operation of the relay protection after disturbances. You also participate as a representative of the customer organization in the daily maintenance activities, planning, procurement and implementation phases of substation projects and, if necessary, support the operation center in challenging disruption situations. We expect you to have extensive and in-depth knowledge of relay protection of the electrical network (400 kV, 110 kV and kj), while you are familiar with the definition and calculation of relay values ​​from your previous work career. We also hope for experience with substation secondary system components, including protective relays and substation automation. You need excellent cooperation and communication skills in the task while working closely with different stakeholders such as maintenance partners, equipment manufacturers, network companies and customers. We also value experience with network calculation and reporting software and enterprise resource planning systems. We expect you to have a university degree suitable for the position and several years of experience working in a substation environment and defining relay values. We are on a journey of change. We are not satisfied with the way we do things now, we raise the bar ourselves - constantly. We want to be even braver and more agile, and that's why we pay special attention to these qualities when recruiting. We cannot succeed without cooperation, and we work closely with both internal stakeholders and external partners. We are looking for people who dare to take ownership and who challenge, question and try new things - doing it skillfully and constructively. We offer you a central role in the development of our capital's electricity distribution network. As a maintenance specialist, you work as part of the Maintenance Group, where you get to work with top professionals in the field. We actively develop our own expertise, we offer the opportunity to participate in industry trainings, we learn from each other and we are happy to share our own views with others as well. In your position, you have flexible working hours and, of course, the opportunity to work remotely or at the office, which is located in Ruohollande WeLand. Come and make a greener future with us! Is there anything left to think about? You can ask your future supervisor Tuomas Murumäi (tel. 050 363 5043/ tuomas.murumaki@helen.fi) for more information about working with us or the task. Did our energies meet? Awesome! You can easily send your application and resume via our recruitment system (helen.fi/rekry), no later than Sun 3 May 2026. Please note that we process applications and proceed with the recruitment of a suitable person already during the application period, so please act quickly. Hope to hear from you soon! Let's join forces!

title: Senior Finance Business Analyst
salary_range: Competitive hourly wage based on Finnish collective agreements.
jobcontent: At Helen, we offer an easier and more uncomplicated everyday life for around 600,000 customers in Finland. In addition to electricity, heat and cooling, we offer solutions for regional and renewable energy and electric transport. We are developing a smarter and cleaner energy system, with which we make sure that our customers can produce, use and save energy while respecting the environment. We systematically reduce our emissions and our goal is to break away from combustion-based energy production by 2040. Come and build the future of clean and flexible energy. We are looking for an experienced and analytical Senior Finance Business Analyst to strengthen Helen Group's financial reporting, analytics and knowledge management. In your role You are responsible for the development of group-level financial reporting, analytics and the financial data model, and you ensure their functionality in strategic guidance, forecasting and decision-making. that business reports are based on common, agreed financial figures. You design and develop financial reports, dashboards and dashboards (Power BI) for the needs of different stakeholders and act as an expert in questions related to the interpretation and use of financial figures. You are responsible for ensuring that reporting, BI and analytics solutions are used in the economy appropriately and in accordance with best practices, and you identify opportunities to develop them, e.g. automation and AI solutions You act as an experienced business sparring partner and bring financial insight to, for example, profitability development, investment evaluation, budgeting and forecasting. You work closely with finance, IT and business operations, ensuring that reporting and analytics solutions support the group's overall management. We expect you to have at least 5-7 years of experience in financial analytics, reporting, data modeling or working with financial forecasting models. Strong knowledge of financial data, dimensions and key figures, as well as the ability to visualize broad entities. Experience with data analytics and reporting tools (e.g. Power BI, SQL, Python, Databricks). Ability to build clear, user-friendly and visually functional dashboards. Ability to change complex financial information into an understandable and decision-making entity. Strong cooperation and communication skills and the ability to act as a credible sparring partner, even for non-financial stakeholders. Interest in the development of financial processes, systems and technologies, as well as self-initiative and development-seeking attitude. You know how to challenge, spar and guide, and you ensure that financial information is correctly used, consistent and relevant for decision-making. You stay on the edge of development and use new technologies and AI solutions without prejudice as part of the continuous development of financial reporting and analytics, for example in support of forecasting, identifying deviations and enhancing reporting processes. We offer you a central and influential role in the development of the entire group's financial visibility and guidance. Your work has a direct impact on how financial information is used in decision-making at different levels of the organization. You get to work in a competent, relaxed and cooperative community of experts, where your point of view is valued and you are given room to develop and influence. At the same time, you are involved in building the future of clean and sustainable energy. You also get to enjoy our various employee benefits, such as employee electricity, a charity work day, the possibility of remote work abroad and a welfare benefit. Our office is located in Ruoholahti's We Land, and we work flexibly with a hybrid model. The salary for the position is determined based on the demands of the position and your skills and experience. translated_content:
Senior Finance Business Analyst

At Helen, we offer an easier and more uncomplicated everyday life for around 600,000 customers in Finland. In addition to electricity, heat and cooling, we offer solutions for regional and renewable energy and electric transport. We are developing a smarter and cleaner energy system, with which we make sure that our customers can produce, use and save energy while respecting the environment. We systematically reduce our emissions and our goal is to break away from combustion-based energy production by 2040. Come and build the future of clean and flexible energy. We are looking for an experienced and analytical Senior Finance Business Analyst to strengthen Helen Group's financial reporting, analytics and knowledge management. In your role You are responsible for the development of group-level financial reporting, analytics and the financial data model, and you ensure their functionality in strategic guidance, forecasting and decision-making. that business reports are based on common, agreed financial figures. You design and develop financial reports, dashboards and dashboards (Power BI) for the needs of different stakeholders and act as an expert in questions related to the interpretation and use of financial figures. You are responsible for ensuring that reporting, BI and analytics solutions are used in the economy appropriately and in accordance with best practices, and you identify opportunities to develop them, e.g. automation and AI solutions You act as an experienced business sparring partner and bring financial insight to, for example, profitability development, investment evaluation, budgeting and forecasting. You work closely with finance, IT and business operations, ensuring that reporting and analytics solutions support the group's overall management. We expect you to have at least 5-7 years of experience in financial analytics, reporting, data modeling or working with financial forecasting models. Strong knowledge of financial data, dimensions and key figures, as well as the ability to visualize broad entities. Experience with data analytics and reporting tools (e.g. Power BI, SQL, Python, Databricks). Ability to build clear, user-friendly and visually functional dashboards. Ability to change complex financial information into an understandable and decision-making entity. Strong cooperation and communication skills and the ability to act as a credible sparring partner, even for non-financial stakeholders. Interest in the development of financial processes, systems and technologies, as well as self-initiative and development-seeking attitude. You know how to challenge, spar and guide, and you ensure that financial information is correctly used, consistent and relevant for decision-making. You stay on the edge of development and use new technologies and AI solutions without prejudice as part of the continuous development of financial reporting and analytics, for example in support of forecasting, identifying deviations and enhancing reporting processes. We offer you a central and influential role in the development of the entire group's financial visibility and guidance. Your work has a direct impact on how financial information is used in decision-making at different levels of the organization. You get to work in a competent, relaxed and cooperative community of experts, where your point of view is valued and you are given room to develop and influence. At the same time, you are involved in building the future of clean and sustainable energy. You also get to enjoy our various employee benefits, such as employee electricity, a charity work day, the possibility of remote work abroad and a welfare benefit. Our office is located in Ruoholahti's We Land, and we work flexibly with a hybrid model. The salary for the position is determined based on the demands of the position and your skills and experience. You can ask your future supervisor Sami Rautiaiin (050 535 4499 / firstname.surname@helen.fi) for more information about working with us or the task. He is (best) reachable by phone on Thursday 16.4 at 15-16, Wed 22.4 at 9-10 and Mon 27.4 at 14-15. Were you interested? Awesome! You can apply through our recruitment system no later than May 4, 2026. Please note that we process applications already during the application period. Hope to hear from you soon!

title: Full-time teacher in the field of electricity and automation
jobcontent:
We are looking for a full-time teacher in the field of electricity and automation for our community-driven team organization from August 1, 2026.

The tasks of a full-time teacher in the field of electricity and automation are mainly focused on teaching the contents of the basic degree and professional degree in the field of electricity and automation. The teacher works as a team member, taking care of the implementation of individual study paths, guiding students in the educational institution and at the workplace, evaluating displays, and planning and developing training together with the rest of the team. In addition, you actively participate in working life cooperation and the development of pedagogical methods. With your own expertise, you are responsible for the day-to-day operations of the electrical industry and customer work.

From the person we are applying for, we require a suitable degree, electrical qualification S2, work experience in the field with an emphasis on electrical installations in buildings in 230/400 V systems, as well as a positive attitude and a desire to develop along with their work. The eligibility requirements for the position of teacher are determined in accordance with Decree 986/1998 on the eligibility of teaching staff. For teachers of vocational subjects in the field of electricity and automation, the qualification can also be produced by a special vocational qualification in the field of electricity and automation (EAT) and at least five years of particularly strong and/or specialized practical skills. 

Success at work is helped by the ability to manage large entities and a bright attitude to work. You have a student-oriented approach that supports professional growth and you are able to develop training and service products in accordance with customers' wishes. You are cooperative and have excellent communication skills. You are a solution-oriented, customer-oriented team player who is enthusiastic about your work. 

We consider experience of teaching at the second level, teamwork, use of Wilma and ItsLearning learning environments, and working life knowledge of the Southwest Häme region to be an advantage.

We ask for work and study certificates from those invited to the interview. Before accepting the position, the selected person must present a criminal record extract and a statement of health in accordance with Act 504/2002. LHKK is a smoke-free workplace.

The position is subject to a 6-month trial period. Salary and terms of employment are determined according to the municipal education sector employment and collective agreement (OVTES). Please note that we use anonymous recruitment and you do not reveal your identity in the application text. In the anonymous recruitment process, those invited to an interview are selected based on the application, educational information and work experience. Any attachments are not available.

The work starts on August 1, 2026 or according to the agreement

The application period ends on May 13, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. We will conduct the interviews on Monday 18.5.26 between 10 am and 2 pm or after 4 pm. Please be prepared to come at short notice.

The Lounais-Häme educational municipality group is a multidisciplinary vocational training provider. Every year, more than 2,000 students study at our educational institution, the Forssa Vocational Institute. In addition, our company Faktia Oy offers comprehensive personnel services to companies, including personnel leasing, and trains several thousand people nationwide in both short and longer trainings. Our group also includes the Fokka Foundation, which produces various employment and youth services. As a responsible operator, we generate vitality in many different ways.

In accordance with our values, we are a bold reformer of working life. We have 180 experts who, like students, we encourage to learn boldly, work together and have a fair attitude. We work as pioneers in self-directed team organization. We are looking for new professionals and brave new builders for our modern community so that we can change the world. We need experts to share experiences and show direction. We have enthusiasm and joy, skill and doing, work and results. We want to make sure that our employees are proud professionals in their field, feel well and enjoy their work. As proof of this, we have been awarded the Great Place To Work certification. See more information about our way of working and our staff benefits here: https://www.lhkk.fi/toihin/meille-toihin/

education director Anu Teppo, anu.teppo@lhkk.fi 040 833 7510
personnel manager Seija Lempiainen seija.lempiainen@lhkk.fi 040 347 4260

handles paperwork related to stock quantity

check the condition of the goods

a nice and close working community

Salary**

jobcontent:
**Warehouse worker for Lohja**

We are looking for a warehouse worker for our client company **CardiRad Oy** to support the growing business.

**Would you like to work in a company in the medical device industry that combines precision, responsibility and smooth everyday operations? In this role, you are an important part of the supply chain - ensuring that the right products reach the right customers on time.**

**CardiRad Oy** is a solvent company founded in 2009 that imports and distributes medical devices and medicines to the Nordic countries and the Baltics. The company represents several international players from Europe, the United States and Asia. https://cardirad.fi/fi

**Your duties include:**

- reception of incoming goods (booking, unloading and shelving)
 
- handling of outgoing goods (booking, packing and shipping)
 
- taking care of the order of the warehouse, participating in inventories
 
- other auxiliary office duties
 

**We expect from you:**

- experience in warehouse work (a few years is enough)
 
- basic IT skills
 
- fluent Finnish and English language skills
 
- independent and vigorous approach to work and diligence
 

A basic degree in logistics is considered an advantage.

**We offer you:**

- permanent work and daytime working hours (at 816)
 
- a nice and close working community
 
- good orientation and support from a supervisor
 
- the opportunity to develop and expand your skills
 
- extensive occupational health care
 
- Smartum benefit
 
- comfortable premises in Lohja, along good transport connections
 

**Salary**

Salary: from €2150/month

**Additional information**

More information about the task is provided by:

Mirja Pursiainen, tel. 044 987 3807 (weekdays from 8 am to 4 pm) or mirja.pursiainen@cardirad.fi

Salme Kortelainen, tel. 044 507 5523 (weekdays 11-12 and 16-17) or rekrytointi@calmen.fi

This recruitment assignment is handled by Calmen HR-palvelut Oy on behalf of its client company. The selected person will be employed directly on the books of CardiRad Oy.

Calme HR services is a recruitment partner that combines the needs of companies and the strengths of experts by genuinely listening. Our goal is to find exactly the right person for this position, who enjoys his job and succeeds in his work in the long term.

Read more: https://www.calmen.fi/ Follow us also on LinkedIn, Instagram and Facebook.

**Additional information**

For more information about the position, please contact: Mirja Pursiainen, tel. 044 987 3807 (on weekdays 8-169) or mirja.pursiainen@cardirad.fi Salme Kortelainen, tel. 044 507 5523 (on weekdays 11-12 and 16-17) or rekrytointi@calmen.fi

**Apply**

Tell us about yourself and your working methods. Send your application and CV **by May 15, 2026**: https://www.calmen.fi/avoimet-tyopaikat

We start interviews already during the application period, and the position is filled as soon as a suitable person is found.
